name: Verdant Health Commission Food Bank
description: Free groceries and fresh produce for families and individuals in need. No appointment needed. Serves all residents of South Snohomish County.
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - Verdant Health Commission is NOT a food bank. It is a Public Hospital District that funds community health services. The actual Lynnwood Food Bank is at 5320 176th St SW, phone (425) 745-1635. This entry should be replaced or removed.

name: Community Transit Access Program
description: Reduced-fare bus passes and paratransit services for seniors, people with disabilities, and low-income residents.
eligibility: Age 65+, disability documentation, or income below 200% federal poverty level
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - All information confirmed via official sources

name: Edmonds School District Family Resource Center
description: Free parenting classes, youth programs, family counseling, and school supply assistance for families with children in Edmonds School District.
eligibility: Families with students in Edmonds School District
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Website URL corrected, other information appears accurate

name: Domestic Violence Services of Snohomish County
description: Emergency shelter, legal advocacy, and support services for survivors of domestic violence. 24/7 crisis hotline available.
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and their children
_QA_STATUS: CORRECTED - Name changed from 'YWCA' to 'Domestic Violence Services of Snohomish County' (DVS). Contact information updated. Website: dvs-snoco.org.

name: Recreation Pavilion Senior Center
description: Daily activities, fitness classes, and social programs for adults 55+. Drop-in welcome.
_QA_FLAG: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW - Name is misleading. This is the 'Recreation Pavilion', a multi-purpose community recreation facility serving ALL ages (pool, fitness room, preschool, etc.), not exclusively a senior center. Consider renaming to 'Recreation Pavilion' or clarifying it serves all ages with senior programs.

name: HealthPoint – Bothell Clinic
description: Primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services. Offers a sliding fee scale and serves insured and uninsured patients from South Snohomish County and surrounding areas.
eligibility: All patients welcome, sliding scale for uninsured
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Updated to correct Bothell clinic location with verified address and phone number.

name: Mountlake Terrace Library (Sno-Isle Libraries)
description: Public library offering free library services, computer access, WiFi, ESL conversation groups, homework help, and literacy programs.
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Updated to official Mountlake Terrace Library (Sno-Isle Libraries). Address verified at 23300 58th Ave W. Email field removed per verification standards. Website updated to main Sno-Isle site.

name: Northwest Immigration Rights Project
description: Free and low-cost immigration legal services including asylum applications, deportation defense, family petitions, and citizenship.
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Information appears accurate. Email updated to Seattle front desk contact. NWIRP operates offices by appointment. Seattle office hours are Mon-Fri 9:30am-12pm and 1pm-4pm.

name: Boys & Girls Clubs of Snohomish County
description: After-school programs, homework help, sports, arts, and mentorship for youth ages 6–18.
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Updated to organization headquarters in Everett. Address verified at 8223 Broadway, Suite 100. Phone and website confirmed. Email removed per verification standards. This is the central office serving Snohomish County; individual club locations available at bgcsc.org/find-a-club/

name: Hopelink Mobile Market
description: Mobile food distribution program providing prepacked boxes of fresh produce and shelf-stable food to communities experiencing food insecurity. Operates at various locations in East King County and North Snohomish County.
accessibility: Mobile service - accessibility varies by location
eligibility: Open to all experiencing food insecurity
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Updated to reflect Hopelink Mobile Market program. This is a mobile food distribution service with rotating locations throughout East King County and North Snohomish County. Email removed per verification standards. Specific distribution sites and schedules available at hopelink.org/mobilemarket.

name: Volunteers of America Western Washington
description: Transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and rental assistance programs for veterans and families experiencing homelessness.
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Updated to main office in Everett. Address verified at 2802 Broadway, Everett, WA 98201. Phone (425) 259-3191 confirmed as main office number. Email and website confirmed. Previous Seattle address was incorrect.

name: Snohomish County Crisis Services
description: 24/7 mental health and substance use crisis support, suicide prevention, and referrals to local resources. Trained behavioral health professionals available by phone or text.
_QA_STATUS: VERIFIED - Updated to official Snohomish County Crisis Services information. Phone number verified as (800) 584-3578. Website confirmed. Email field removed as no official program email was verified. Service operates 24/7 with trained behavioral health professionals available by phone or text.
